title: ps -a lists lots of errors opening create-config
body:
```
$ rpm-ostree status -b && rpm -q podman
State: idle; auto updates disabled
Deployments:
● ostree://fedora-27:fedora/27/x86_64/workstation
                   Version: 27.97 (2018-03-24 22:12:25)
                BaseCommit: a29a94114388694e5bcb44e00f917151575214e6257fd7cd3a5137cbde8f636f
              GPGSignature: Valid signature by 860E19B0AFA800A1751881A6F55E7430F5282EE4
           LayeredPackages: ansible emacs fuse-sshfs git-evtag gstreamer1-plugin-openh264 keepassx krb5-workstation libvirt-client mozilla-openh264 oci-kvm-hook opensc origin-clients pcsc-lite-ccid podman
                            powerline strace tmux vagrant-libvirt virt-manager xsel ykclient ykpers
podman-0.3.3-2.gitb47fc0d.fc27.x86_64
$ podman ps -a
ERRO[0000] couldn't get some ps information, error getting artifact "0889b1c1cdabcfc206ee18b344f02ece8146c8b1b227948c526559b3b233d205": open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/0889b1c1cdabcfc206ee18b344f02ece8146c8b1b227948c526559b3b233d205/userdata/artifacts/create-config: no such file or directory 
ERRO[0000] couldn't get some ps information, error getting artifact "10d26155f0489ef9471f6308fc7d6e366edcc25c71b2efedf1268ebf9082cf46": open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/10d26155f0489ef9471f6308fc7d6e366edcc25c71b2efedf1268ebf9082cf46/userdata/artifacts/create-config: no such file or directory 
...
```

But then it proceeds to list containers and exits with code `0`.  This system has been upgraded from old 0.2 podman etc.  I have no problem nuking all local state if this is likely a legacy data/upgrade issue.

comments.body:
The state should be fine. The individual containers in question might need to be recreated to make it stop throwing those errors. though. It shouldn't impact your ability to work with the containers, though - this is just `ps` not locating some extra information it needs for full display.

We should also drop that down to WARN level before we get CRI-O integration. We can't get everything `ps` wants from CRI-O's containers, so that warning will be popping up a lot if you do a `podman ps` on a system with CRI-O running.
Will document as a known issue
I believe this is fixed.  Please reopen if this is still an issue.
